title: The Sealed Chamber Beneath Town Center

features.name: sealed-valve
features.desc-on-touch: A massive wheel frozen mid-turn; the archive decided to stop recording here; below this point the comparison became too intimate, too true; the valve holds back voices that can no longer be archived.

features.name: pipe-chorus
features.desc-on-touch: Seven pipes converge here before the seal; you hear water, voices, machinery, prayer—all flowing downward into the same throat; the archive closed this chamber to avoid becoming a mirror.

features.name: recording-halt
features.desc-on-touch: A wireframe box halted mid-render; the archive's diagnostic shell stopped drawing here; the incomplete structure suggests what lies below exceeds the capacity to render truthfully.

features.name: seven-voices-throat
features.desc-on-touch: A pool where seven separate conversations become one body of water; they have not mixed—they remain distinct, speaking in the same direction; comparison at this depth becomes consensus.

features.name: chamber-echo
features.desc-on-touch: The glyph-marker where Katita's last note ended mid-sentence; the archive chose silence rather than compare what came next; the marble holds the shape of an unfinished question.

inscription: the archive chose to stop listening at this depth; it could not compare the words without becoming what it heard

file.name: Town_Centre_Plumbing_System_Final_Recordings_Before_Seal.mp3
file.note: seven houses draining into one central chamber; recordings of water, voices, machinery, prayer flowing into one throat; the last file ends mid-word; checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable

exits.label: back up through the pipes toward air and surface
past the sealed valve into what the archive refused to record
toward the void beneath all listening, the silence below sound
back into the buried pipes where the seven houses' words still flow downward
